items.headline: Baseten raises a $1.5B Series F at a $13B valuation (two tranches $13B/$11B) led by Altimeter, Conviction, Spark — 4th raise in 18 months, ~20x YoY revenue, >1B inference requests/day
OpenAI previews GPT-5.6 family (Sol/Terra/Luna) to ~20 government-approved companies; Sol sets SOTA on Terminal-Bench 2.1, strongest safety stack to date
Anthropic accuses Alibaba (Qwen lab) of illicitly accessing Claude via ~25,000 fake accounts / 28.8M queries (Apr–Jun) for 'adversarial distillation'; briefs White House & senators
Trump administration (Commerce/Lutnick) authorizes limited re-release of Anthropic's Mythos 5 to ~100 trusted US firms and federal agencies; Fable 5 stays barred
Markets (wk of Jun 26): Nasdaq -4.5% (QQQ ~$707), S&P -1.9% to ~7,354, 10Y ~4.38% on tech rotation + OpenAI IPO-delay report + Apple/Microsoft price hikes; Micron +16%
CRED raises $900M Series H at $4.5B led by Meta (minority stake); founder Kunal Shah steps back from CEO to lead WhatsApp internationally, Miten Sampat interim CEO
General Intuition raises $320M Series A at $2.3B led by Khosla Ventures — world models trained on Medal gameplay clips; already raising Series B
Stark Defence raises €500M at €3.5B+ co-led by Sequoia and Founders Fund — Berlin drone/defense startup up from ~$500M (Aug 2025) in under a year
a16z leads seed for Mirendil (autonomous AI-for-R&D models); founding team from Google, Anthropic, xAI
MIT 'AI and Society Forum' (June 23): David Autor reframes AI's labor threat as expertise scarcity, not job loss
Claude Code late-June release notes — /rewind, finer auto-mode controls, ~37% lower streaming CPU, MCP/OAuth reliability fixes

Markets (wk of Jun 29–Jul 3, holiday-shortened; closed Fri Jul 3): Dow record, Nasdaq lags on AI/semi valuation worries (Nvidia/AMD/Broadcom/Micron), QQQ ~$725, 10Y ~4.48%, softer June jobs report cut Sept hike odds to ~50%
items.source: Sunday Guardian / Federal Reserve H.15 / Trading Economics

items.headline: Together AI raises $800M Series C at $8.3B valuation (led by Aramco Ventures) — GPU-cloud platform for training/serving open-source models
Joulent raises $1.75B (National Grid Ventures) — energy infrastructure for AI and compute-intensive industries; the week's biggest round

Anthropic launches Claude Science (June 30) — AI workbench for researchers/pharma (protein structure prediction, 60+ scientific databases) and announces it will run its own preclinical drug-discovery programs for neglected/rare diseases
US lifts export controls June 30; Claude Fable 5 returns globally July 1 across Claude platform/Code/Cowork; Anthropic adds new jailbreak classifier blocking the targeted technique >99%
Quantum Systems raises $1.2B Series D (~$8B valuation; Blackstone, Airbus, Advent) — Munich defense-tech, long-endurance drones + multi-domain autonomy
Twelve Labs raises $100M Series B at $1B+ (NEA, Naver Ventures) — video indexing/analysis AI for enterprise archives
8090 Solutions raises $135M led by Salesforce Ventures — AI-agent platform that develops enterprise applications end-to-end
Google ships Gemma 4 12B (runs locally on 16GB laptop, unified vision + native voice) and adds computer-use to Gemini 3.5 Flash for cross-device agents
Venice raises $65M at $1B valuation (Dragonfly Capital) — privacy-first platform for private access to AI models
H1 2026 seed structure: median seed pre-money +19% to ~$16M while seed volume -29% YoY (flight to quality); AI startups carry 1.3–1.6x valuation premium
items.source: Carta State of Pre-Seed Q1 2026 / VC Beast

items.headline: Embodied AI reaching production scale: Figure producing Figure 03 at ~1 robot/hour (BotQ), Tesla converting Fremont lines for Optimus Gen 3 (summer ramp), China 150+ humanoid firms vs ~20 US

Enterprise-agent spending gap: agent-software spend forecast ~$206B 2026 (+139% YoY), Gartner sees 40% of enterprise apps embedding task-specific agents by year-end, but only a small minority production-ready

Inc./Medium on AI + human expertise: LinkedIn 2026 Skills Report (Soren Kaplan, June 26) — fastest-growing skills split into AI fluency + human judgment; Papalini Medium argues AI commoditizes ~70% of execution, remaining 30% (judgment/taste) is the value
20VC late-June episode (June 29) on AI M&A/secondary-market frenzy — reported nine-figure acquisition and trillion-dollar-secondary numbers for marquee AI names

Markets (wk of Jul 6-10): S&P 500 closed Fri Jul 10 at ~7,575 (+1.2% wk), QQQ ~$726, 10Y ~4.55%; mega-cap tech rebound (Meta ~+15%, Nvidia roadmap reassurance), softer June payrolls (+57k), hawkish June FOMC minutes keep July hold base case
items.source: CNBC / Motley Fool / BBN Times / ETF Trends

items.headline: SK Hynix debuts on US markets Fri Jul 10 up ~13%, raising $26.5B — the largest-ever US IPO by a foreign company (topping Alibaba's 2014 $25B); trades under SKHY

Fed Chair Kevin Warsh names a16z co-founder Marc Andreessen to co-lead a new external 'Productivity and Jobs' task force (with Charles I. Jones, Asha Sharma) on AI's effect on employment/productivity; recommendations due end of 2026
Norm AI raises $120M Series C led by Khosla Ventures at $1.2B valuation, hits unicorn status — agentic legal/compliance AI (~$260M raised in under 3 years)

Agility Robotics (Digit warehouse robot; CEO Peggy Johnson) to go public via SPAC merger with Churchill Capital Corp XI at ~$2.5B, raising >$620M — first pure-play humanoid robotics company on US public markets
Oratomic raises $300M Series A for quantum computing, co-led by ARCH Venture Partners, Spark Capital, Khosla Ventures (Bezos Expeditions, Index, General Catalyst, Bain participating)
SpaceXAI releases Grok 4.5, described by Musk as an 'Opus-class' model co-built with Cursor; priced $2/$6 per Mtok, available in Grok Build, Cursor, and SpaceXAI console
Prime Intellect raises $130M Series A at $1B valuation (led by Radical Ventures; Nvidia Ventures, Intel Capital, Dell, Iconiq) — agentic AI infrastructure platform reaches unicorn
Monogram (Coursera co-founder Eren Bali) raises $40M seed led by DST Global and Lux Capital — voice-driven app that generates entire interactive UIs from queries (generative UI)

Tour de France 2026: Pogačar seizes yellow with a Stage 6 Tourmalet attack, leads Vingegaard by 2:42 through Stage 7 (del Toro 3rd); peloton tech trends toward ultra-narrow bars and short (160mm) cranks
Almost 90 startups crossed $1B in H1 2026 — more than double last year's pace, AI-driven (standouts: Bezos-linked Prometheus $41B, UK Ineffable Intelligence $5.1B)

Waymo announces driverless-ride expansion to San Diego, Las Vegas, Tampa, Denver (targets 1M weekly trips); Tesla launches unsupervised robotaxi in Miami; China's Momenta lists in Hong Kong raising ~$751M (BlackRock, Fidelity, GIC, Mercedes, BYD cornerstones)
Paradigm raises $1.2B fourth fund and rebrands as a 'technical frontier' investor (AI + robotics alongside crypto); Chemistry raising $500M second fund — new-fund formation pivots to frontier tech

Nature: 'Is AI ruining our skills? Early results are in — and they're not good' — first evidence of AI-driven deskilling (med/CS students worse when tested without AI; 77% physicians, 70% nurses worry); SciAm and APA Monitor ran companion analyses

Anthropic brings Claude Cowork to cloud, iOS, Android, and web (Max subscribers first, from Jul 7) — sessions run remotely across devices with background/scheduled tasks; usage limits doubled through Aug 5
